query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Integrity', 'Adherence to moral and ethical principles', 'Critics argued that the plan might integrity the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intellect', 'The faculty of thinking, judging, abstract reasoning, etc', 'The essay highlights the intellect of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intelligible', 'Capable of being understood, comprehensible, clear', 'The essay highlights the intelligible of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intemperance', 'Lack of moderation or temperance, excess', 'The essay highlights the intemperance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intension', 'Intensification, increase in degree', 'Scholars noted a striking intension between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intention', 'A course of action that a person intends to follow', 'Critics argued that the plan might intention the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inter', 'To bury in a grave', 'Critics argued that the plan might inter the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interact', 'To act  one upon another', 'Over time, the community learned to interact its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intercede', 'To intervene on behalf of another person; to mediate or plead', 'Critics argued that the plan might intercede the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intercept', 'To stop, deflect or divert (something in progress or motion)', 'Critics argued that the plan might intercept the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intercession', 'An act or instance of interceding', 'Scholars noted a striking intercession between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intercessor', 'A person who intercedes, a mediator', 'Her intercessor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interdependent', 'Mutually reliant on each other; interconnected', 'The reviewer described the tone as interdependent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interdict', 'Any prohibitory decree of a court or administrative officer', 'Her interdict became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interim', 'An intervening time, transitional, meantime', 'The essay highlights the interim of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interject', 'To insert something between other things', 'Critics argued that the plan might interject the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interlocutor', 'Someone engaged in conversation', 'The essay highlights the interlocutor of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interloper', 'A person who interferes or meddles in the affairs of others', 'Her interloper became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interlude', 'An intervening episode, space, period, etc', 'Her interlude became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intermediate', 'To mediate, to be an intermediate', 'Over time, the community learned to intermediate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interminable', 'Existing or occurring without interruption or end, ceaseless', 'What seemed interminable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intermission', 'A break between two performances or sessions (concert, etc)', 'Scholars noted a striking intermission between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intermit', 'To interrupt, to stop or cease temporarily, to suspend', 'Critics argued that the plan might intermit the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intermittent', 'Occurring at irregular intervals', 'The passage presents a intermittent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Internecine', 'Of or pertaining to conflict or struggle within a group', 'The essay highlights the internecine of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interpolate', 'To introduce (something additional) between other things', 'The author hoped the new policy would interpolate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interpose', 'To insert something (or oneself) between other things', 'The author hoped the new policy would interpose tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interpretation', 'An explanation or understanding of something', '“His interpretation of the poem differed from hers.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interpreter', 'One who translates the words of a speaker to the audience', 'Over time, the community learned to interpreter its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interrogate', 'Question aggressively or formally', 'Over time, the community learned to interrogate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intersect', 'To cross, to cut', 'Over time, the community learned to intersect its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intersperse', 'To mix irregularly something with something else', 'The author hoped the new policy would intersperse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Interstice', 'A small opening or space between objects', 'Her interstice became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intertwine', 'Closely connected or twisted together', '“Their lives became intertwined after years of friendship.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intervale', 'A lowly tract of land along a river', 'Scholars noted a striking intervale between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intervene', 'To come between, or to be between, persons or things', 'Critics argued that the plan might intervene the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intestacy', 'The state of dying without having made a valid will', 'Scholars noted a striking intestacy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intestate', 'To be without a valid will', 'Over time, the community learned to intestate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intestine', 'The lower part of the alimentary canal', 'Scholars noted a striking intestine between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intimacy', 'The state of being intimate', 'Her intimacy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intimation', 'The act of intimating, also, the thing intimated', 'Her intimation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intimidate', 'To make timid or fearful, to inspire or affect with fear', 'Over time, the community learned to intimidate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intolerable', 'Not capable of being borne or endured, insufferable', 'The passage presents a intolerable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intoxicant', 'An intoxicating agent, as alcohol or drugs', 'Her intoxicant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intoxicate', 'To stupefy by doping with chemical substances like alcohol', 'Critics argued that the plan might intoxicate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intracellular', 'Inside or within a cell', 'The essay highlights the intracellular of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intractable', 'Hard to control or deal with', 'Critics argued that the plan might intractable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intramural', 'Pertaining to something within one institution', 'The author hoped the new policy would intramural tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intransigent', 'Unwilling to change one’s views or agree', '“The two sides remained intransigent during negotiations.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intrepid', 'Fearless; adventurous', 'The essay highlights the intrepid of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
